refnamediv.refpurpose: Deep-copy an object (optionally in a geometric pattern) and place the copies in the database with automatically generated names.

listitem.para: Display help and exit.
Starting increment used when generating copy names. The first numeric run found in each object name is incremented by this value; if no number is present the increment is appended. Default is 100.
Move the naming cursor to the next numeric run in object names. May be repeated: -c -c targets the third number, -c -c -c the fourth, etc. Default targets the first number.
Controls how deeply the copy hierarchy is constructed.

top (default for pattern modes) — creates a lightweight wrapper combination whose single member is the original object with the placement matrix embedded. No deep copy is performed; the source geometry is shared.

regions — copies every combination from the top of the tree down to and including the first region encountered on each path. Primitives below the region boundary are not copied — they are shared between the original and the clone. New region idents are assigned automatically so that each copy is distinguishable during raytracing. The placement matrix is distributed into the leaf arcs inside each region (rather than stored at the top-level arc). This is the C++ equivalent of the Tcl copy_obj + apply_mat -regions workflow.
primitives — performs a full recursive copy of the entire hierarchy down to individual primitives and absorbs each placement matrix directly into the copied primitive parameters.

Before producing the final clone, internally flatten any matrices that hang above the combination members of the source object (equivalent to running xpush on a temporary copy). Useful when -t / -r do not behave as expected because of existing instance matrices in the hierarchy. This replaces the xclone workaround.
Collect all top-level clone results into a new combination named group_name.

Apply a name substitution to every generated object name. The first occurrence of the string sstr in each source object's name is replaced with rstr before the numeric uniquification step. For example, clone -s tank tread tank.r01 produces a clone named tread.r101 (or the next available variant). When no occurrence of sstr is found in a particular name the name is used as-is.
Number of copies to make. Default is 1.
Per-copy translation delta (model units). Conflicts with -a.
Per-copy rotation in degrees about the X, Y, and Z axes. Conflicts with -b.
Rotation pivot point for -r and -b. Default is 0 0 0.
Total translation distance divided evenly across n copies. Conflicts with -t.
Total rotation in degrees divided evenly across n copies. Conflicts with -r.
Create a mirror copy reflected about the plane perpendicular to axis ('x', 'y', or 'z') at distance along that axis.

refsection.title: Rectangular-grid pattern (--rect)
refsection.para: Place copies at every point of a 3-D grid aligned with three user-supplied direction vectors. This replaces the pattern_rect Tcl procedure for all three depth modes (top, regions, and primitives).

listitem.para: Grid direction vectors (need not be unit vectors). Defaults are the X, Y, and Z axes.
Number of steps and step size (model units) along --xdir. Alternatively supply an explicit list with --lx "v1 v2 ...".
Equivalent parameters for Y and Z directions.

refsection.title: Spherical pattern (--sph)
refsection.para: Place copies at spherical-coordinate grid positions (azimuth × elevation × radius). This replaces the pattern_sph Tcl procedure for all three depth modes (top, regions, and primitives).

listitem.para: Origin of the spherical grid (model-space point where radius = 0 maps to). Default is 0 0 0.
Local centre of the source object, used as the pivot when --rotaz or --rotel are active. Default is 0 0 0.
Rotate each copy so that it faces the azimuth angle of its position.
Rotate each copy to face its elevation angle.
Starting azimuth (degrees), elevation (degrees), and radius (model units). Defaults are 0, −90, and 0.
Number of azimuth steps and delta per step (degrees). Alternatively: --laz "v1 v2 ...".
Number of elevation steps and delta (degrees), or --lel "v1 v2 ...".
Number of radius steps and delta (model units), or --lr "v1 v2 ...".

refsection.title: Cylindrical pattern (--cyl)
refsection.para: Place copies at positions on a cylinder defined by azimuth, radius, and height. This replaces the pattern_cyl Tcl procedure for all three depth modes (top, regions, and primitives).

listitem.para: Base-centre of the cylinder in model space. Default is 0 0 0.
Unit vector along the cylinder axis. Default is 0 0 1 (Z axis).
Radial direction that corresponds to azimuth = 0. Must be perpendicular to --height-dir. Default is 1 0 0.
Local centre of the source object, used as pivot when --rot is active. Default is 0 0 0.
Rotate each copy to face the tangential direction at its azimuth position.
Starting azimuth (degrees), radius (model units), and height (model units) along the cylinder axis. All default to 0.
Azimuth step count and delta (degrees), or --laz "v1 v2 ...".
Radius step count and delta (model units), or --lr "v1 v2 ...".
Height step count and delta (model units), or --lh "v1 v2 ...".

refsection.title: DESCRIPTION
refsection.para: clone copies one or more database objects and places the copies with automatically generated unique names. Names are generated by incrementing the first numeric run found in the source object's name (configurable with -c and -i); if no number is present the increment value is appended.
In its default (linear) mode, clone produces -n copies arranged by the supplied translation (-t / -a) and/or rotation (-r / -b) increments. The -m option produces a single mirrored copy instead.
Three pattern modes produce a regular arrangement of copies without requiring manual calculation of individual displacements:

listitem.para: --rect — rectangular (Cartesian) grid.
--sph — spherical grid (azimuth × elevation × radius).
--cyl — cylindrical grid (azimuth × radius × height).

refsection.para: In all three pattern modes, each position in the grid yields one clone of every source object listed on the command line. The --depth option controls the copy strategy: top shares geometry via a wrapper combination, regions copies the combination hierarchy down to regions (sharing primitives) and distributes the placement matrix into region leaf arcs, and primitives produces a fully independent deep copy with the matrix baked into primitives.
The database must be in v5 format. Use dbupgrade to convert a v4 database before running clone.
If you specify conflicting copy-count options (-a, -b, -n), the last n value supplied wins.

refsection.title: EXAMPLES
refsection.para: Make three copies of wheel translated 100 mm apart along X, collecting them into a group: clone -n 3 -t 100 0 0 -g wheels wheel

Full spherical shell of sensors at two radii with five elevation bands of 12 azimuth positions each: clone --sph --naz 12 --daz 30 --nel 5 --del 30 --nr 2 --dr 25 \ --start-el -60 --start-r 100 -g sensors sensor
3 × 3 rectangular grid of panels spaced 200 mm apart: clone --rect --nx 3 --dx 200 --ny 3 --dy 200 -g panel_array panel
Clone with xpush to absorb pre-existing instance matrices: clone --xpush -t 50 0 0 assembly

refsection.title: BUG REPORTS
refsection.para: Reports of bugs or problems should be submitted via electronic mail to devs@brlcad.org
